senses.definition: to state that one wants to see or speak to someone specific

examples.x: I'll ask for the CEO when I call the corporate office to discuss the partnership proposal.
The client asked for the head of the design department to oversee their project.
The employee asked for the company director to discuss career development opportunities.
She asked for you earlier; she wanted to discuss the project.
We asked for the principal regarding the event arrangements.

senses.definition: to admit defeat and retreat from a position or claim when met with resistance or pressure

examples.x: The teacher didn't back down from enforcing the classroom rules.
The government didn't back down from the controversial policy.
The protesters vowed not to back down until their demands were met.
She didn't back down from her position even when confronted with criticism.

senses.definition: to firmly trust in the goodness or value of something

examples.x: We should all believe in the power of kindness to make the world a better place.
It's crucial for society to believe in equality for a just and inclusive future.
In challenging times, it's important to believe in the resilience of the human spirit.
She always believed in the goodness of people, no matter the circumstances.
I don't believe in discrimination based on gender in the workplace.
He doesn't believe in the imposition of strict dress codes in schools.

senses.definition: to cause something to happen, especially something undesirable or unpleasant

examples.x: Lack of sleep can bring on a range of health problems.
The economic downturn brought on financial difficulties for many businesses.
He was suffering from stress brought on by overwork.
Lack of proper preparation can bring on unexpected challenges during a project.

senses.definition: to mention a particular subject

examples.x: He brought up the topic of technology during the discussion.
She brought up an interesting fact from earlier in the lecture.
She always brings up her hometown in conversations.
Could you bring up your concerns at the next meeting?

senses.definition: to cancel what has been planned

examples.x: We might need to call off the picnic if it keeps raining.
The event was called off at the last minute due to low attendance.
The authorities had to call off the festival due to security concerns.

senses.definition: to go faster and reach someone or something that is ahead
to feel happy and satisfied

examples.x: I've been feeling down, but I noticed I tend to cheer up when the sun is shining.
Whenever I hear that song, I can't help but cheer up.
The atmosphere at the party started to cheer up as more people arrived.
Just spending time with friends can make you cheer up unexpectedly.

senses.definition: to make oneself neat or clean

examples.x: The custodian worked diligently to clean up the spilled coffee in the office breakroom.
After the party, everyone pitched in to clean up the decorations and leftover food.
The environmental activists are urging residents to help clean up local parks and green spaces.

senses.definition: to discover, meet, or find someone or something by accident

examples.x: While cleaning out the attic, I came across an old box of photographs from my childhood.
During my walk in the park, I came across an injured bird and decided to take it to a wildlife rescue center.
I was browsing through a bookstore when I came across a novel that caught my attention.
While researching for my project, I came across an interesting article that provided valuable insights.
I didn't expect to come across an old friend from high school at the conference, but it was a pleasant surprise.

senses.definition: to be brought up or mentioned in a conversation or discussion

examples.x: I hope my achievements will come up during the performance review.
The issue of funding for the project came up at the team meeting.
We need to ensure that all relevant points come up in the negotiations.
Her name came up when we were discussing potential team leaders.
It's important that everyone's concerns come up during the town hall meeting.

senses.definition: to visit a place or someone briefly, often without a prior arrangement

examples.x: Feel free to drop by my office if you have any questions.
We're having a small gathering tonight; you should drop by if you're free.
While you're in the neighborhood, why not drop by for a cup of coffee?
I'll be in the area later; can I drop by your place for a quick chat?
Friends often drop by unexpectedly, turning an ordinary day into a pleasant visit.

senses.definition: to eat in a restaurant, etc. rather than at one's home

examples.x: On special occasions, the family likes to eat out at their favorite restaurant.
After a busy day, she decided to treat herself and eat out instead of cooking.
The couple enjoys exploring new cuisines and frequently chooses to eat out.
Instead of packing lunch, they decided to eat out and try the nearby cafes.
When traveling, it's common for tourists to eat out and experience local cuisine.

senses.definition: to eventually reach or find oneself in a particular place, situation, or condition, often unexpectedly or as a result of circumstances

examples.x: After several detours, we ended up at the beautiful hidden beach we had heard about.
Sometimes life takes unexpected turns, and you might end up in a career you hadn't initially considered.

senses.definition: to fall or break into pieces as a result of being in an extremely bad condition

examples.x: The neglected house, worn down by years of weathering, finally began to fall apart, with sections of the roof and walls collapsing.
The old book, its pages yellowed and brittle, seemed ready to fall apart with each turn.
After the earthquake, many buildings in the city started to fall apart, posing a significant safety risk.
The neglected vintage car, left in the elements for decades, eventually began to fall apart, rusting and deteriorating.
The poorly constructed furniture quickly started to fall apart, with joints loosening and pieces breaking off.

senses.definition: to find the answer to a question or problem

examples.x: After hours of studying, she finally managed to figure out the complex math problem.
The detective worked diligently to figure out the mystery behind the unusual events.
It took some time, but he was able to figure out how to operate the new software.
Students often collaborate to figure out challenging assignments during group projects.
The team brainstormed to figure out the best strategy for the upcoming competition.

senses.definition: to temporarily do a person's job while they are away or are unable to do it themselves
to have a friendly or good relationship with someone or something

examples.x: Despite their differences, they manage to get along and work as a team.
Siblings don't always get along, but they share a deep bond.

senses.definition: to give something as a gift or donation to someone

examples.x: She decided to give away her old clothes to a local charity.
The school organized a book drive to give away textbooks to underprivileged students.
The company is going to give away free samples of their new product at the event.

senses.definition: to thoroughly review, examine, or check something

examples.x: Please go over your essay for any grammatical errors before submitting it.
The accountant will go over the financial statements to ensure accuracy.
It's important to go over the terms and conditions before signing the contract.
The teacher asked the students to go over their homework assignments.
We need to go over the details of the project to make sure nothing is missed.

senses.definition: to ask someone to wait briefly or pause for a moment

examples.x: The doctor asked the patient to hang on while they reviewed the test results.
Can you hang on for a moment?
Hang on a second; I need to grab my coat before we leave.
She told her friend to hang on while she finished a quick task.
He told his team to hang on while he reviewed the final details of the project.

senses.definition: to spend much time in a specific place or with someone particular

examples.x: We're going to hang out at the park this afternoon.
I like to hang out with my friends at the local coffee shop.
Let's hang out at my place and watch a movie tonight.
They often hang out by the beach during the summer.
Do you want to hang out after school and grab a bite to eat?

senses.definition: to intentionally exclude someone or something

examples.x: The author left out a crucial plot point, leaving readers confused and unsatisfied.
The parents decided to leave out their teenage son from their decision-making process, causing him to feel frustrated and unheard.
The scientist left out a key variable from the experiment, invalidating the results.
The teacher left out my essay from the grading stack, resulting in a delay in receiving my feedback.
I’ll leave out the technical terms to make the explanation simpler.

senses.definition: to understand something, often with effort

examples.x: How did you make out the answer to that riddle?
I couldn't quite make out the reason for her sudden behavior.
Can you help me make out what's driving him to behave this way?
I am still trying to make out the purpose of this exercise.
Can you make out the logic behind his decision?
She struggled to make out the meaning of the complex instructions.
I could not make out what he meant by his comment.

senses.definition: to no longer be alive

examples.x: The community mourned when they heard that the beloved teacher had passed away.
I just found out that my childhood friend passed away in an accident.
She passed away peacefully in her sleep.
We were saddened to hear that the town's oldest resident passed away at the age of 105.
My grandfather passed away last year after a long illness.

senses.definition: to postpone an appointment or arrangement
to eliminate an option or idea from consideration due to it appearing impossible to realize

examples.x: After careful examination, they ruled out the idea of a merger as it didn't align with their long-term goals.
The doctor had to rule out the possibility of a severe illness before suggesting further tests.

senses.definition: to attend to a specific task or responsibility

examples.x: He will see to the safety regulations before the project begins.
I'll see to the dishes after dinner.
Could you see to the paperwork while I'm away?
I need to see to the repairs in the house.
The manager will see to the customer complaints promptly.

senses.definition: to arrive at an event or appointment where one is expected

examples.x: The musician showed up for the concert well-prepared and enthusiastic.
The speaker always shows up at conferences with insightful presentations.
The team captain consistently shows up for practice, setting an example for others.
The volunteers showed up early to help set up for the community event.
The professor consistently shows up for office hours to assist students.

senses.definition: to put or organize things in a tidy or systematic way

examples.x: After the move, she spent the weekend sorting out her belongings in the new apartment.
The librarian decided to sort out the books on the shelves to make it easier for patrons to find them.
He began to sort out the files on his computer to create a more efficient digital workspace.

senses.definition: to get rid of what is not needed or wanted anymore

examples.x: I will throw away the old magazines cluttering the living room.
She decided to throw away the broken toys.
They were reluctant to throw away sentimental belongings.

senses.definition: to decline an invitation, request, or offer

examples.x: The landlord turned down the rent increase, mindful of tenant affordability.
The city council turned down the rezoning proposal, respecting community concerns.

senses.definition: to interrupt someone to say something

examples.x: He constantly puts in with jokes, even when the conversation is serious.
It's frustrating when people put in with irrelevant comments during important discussions.
Every time I try to speak, someone always puts in with their opinion.
During the discussion, Lisa felt compelled to put in and share her experience.
I was explaining the plan when Jane put in her thoughts.

senses.definition: to entirely remove something

examples.x: The educational program is designed to wipe out illiteracy in underprivileged communities.
The campaign against drug abuse hopes to wipe out addiction from our society.
I accidentally wiped out all the files on my computer.

senses.definition: to stack things on top of each other

examples.x: She piled up the dirty dishes in the sink.
The workers piled up the bricks to prepare for construction.
At the construction site, workers piled up sandbags to prevent flooding.

senses.definition: to make something stop burning or shining
to escape from a person who is holding one

examples.x: She managed to break away from her captor and run to safety.
The child tried to break away from his mother's grasp and explore the park on his own.
The horse suddenly broke away from its rider and galloped freely across the field.
The prisoner attempted to break away from the guards during the chaos.
The protesters tried to break away from the police blockade and continue their march.

senses.definition: to support someone or a team or hope that they will succeed

examples.x: She'll root for her favorite soccer team at the match this weekend.
The whole town will root for their local hero in the upcoming championship.
He roots for his best friend during the intense chess competition.
We'll always root for you in your endeavors and challenges.
The fans will root for the athlete, no matter the outcome of the race.

senses.definition: to leave somewhere having a certain impression or feeling

examples.x: We came away with the impression that all was not well with their marriage.
We don't come away from all this with wildly genial feelings towards the author. .
After visiting the museum, I came away with a deep appreciation for art history.

senses.definition: to suddenly and intensely enter a particular emotional or mental state

examples.x: Upon hearing the tragic news, she flew into a fit of uncontrollable tears.
The teacher flew into a rage when she saw the students cheating.
The fan flew into a frenzy when his team scored the winning goal.
She flew into a panic when she realized she had lost her wallet.
The unexpected gift made her fly into a state of delight.

senses.definition: to secretly store something in a place in order to use it later

examples.x: The pirate captain stashed away the treasure on a remote island.
He stashed away his favorite snacks so his siblings wouldn't find them.
The detective carefully stashed away the evidence to ensure its confidentiality.

senses.definition: to use a particular situation, resources, or opportunity effectively to gain some benefit

examples.x: She capitalized on her experience to get a better paying job.
The musician tried to capitalize on the popularity of a viral song by releasing an album.
They plan to capitalize on the current trend and launch a related product.
She wanted to capitalize on her recent success by starting her own business.
The athlete aimed to capitalize on her strong performance to secure endorsement deals.

senses.definition: to retrieve an item from a location where it was left

examples.x: I need to pick up my dry cleaning before the shop closes.
I need to pick up the books I reserved from the library later today.
